preview_text:
Argentina enters as a decisive favorite with a model-derived win probability of 80%, while Jordan's chances of victory stand at just 4%. The underdog faces a steep climb despite competing on neutral ground; the 76-percentage-point gap reflects a substantial asymmetry in expected performance.

The draw emerges as the secondary outcome at 16%, making it a plausible result but one well below the likelihood of an Argentina win. The spread across outcomes—a commanding lead for the favorite, a slim but nonzero upset window, and a moderate draw probability—suggests a match where Argentina is expected to control proceedings without guaranteeing a decisive finish.

The model and sportsbook consensus align closely on Argentina's win probability, separated by just 1 percentage point. The market assigns slightly higher odds to a Jordan upset (6% vs. 4%) and slightly lower odds to a draw (13% vs. 16%). These gaps fall within typical variance and indicate broad agreement on match structure. Polymarket pricing leans slightly more bullish on Argentina but sits within the same general frame.
